title: Map: The village park, now and 10 years ago
description: The map shows the village park, now and 10 years ago. Summarize the information by selecting and reporting the main features, and make comparisons where relevant.
guide_html:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT BÀI

Village park (10 years ago vs now)
1\. Giải thích đề

Sơ đồ cho thấy sự thay đổi của một công viên làng trong vòng 10 năm.

→ Dạng: map – changes over time

2\. Introduction (paraphrase)

Giữ: village park – 10 years ago – now

Dùng:

  • shows → illustrates / compares
  • now → at present / currently
3\. Overview (ý chính)
  • Công viên được hiện đại hoá và bổ sung nhiều tiện ích
  • Các yếu tố tự nhiên (pond, woods) biến mất
  • Xuất hiện thêm khu vui chơi, cafe và đường đi xe đạp
4\. Chia BODY
BODY 1 – Khu trung tâm & thể thao
  • Tennis courts:
  • 2 → 4 (mở rộng)
  • Football pitch: giữ nhưng thay đổi vị trí nhẹ
  • Pond \+ woods → bị loại bỏ
  • Thêm picnic area

→ Ý: chuyển từ không gian tự nhiên sang khu giải trí có tổ chức

BODY 2 – Khu tiện ích & dịch vụ
  • Car park:
  • 20 → 50 cars (mở rộng đáng kể)
  • Children’s play area:
  • được giữ và mở rộng
  • thêm soft play area
  • Thêm:
  • Toilets
  • Cafe
  • Cycle path (xung quanh công viên)

→ Ý: tăng mạnh dịch vụ phục vụ người dùng

5\. Gợi ý từ vựng
  • be expanded → được mở rộng
  • be replaced by → được thay thế
  • be removed → bị loại bỏ
  • be introduced → được bổ sung
  • recreational facilities → tiện ích giải trí
  • natural features → yếu tố tự nhiên

The diagrams compare the layout of a village park now with its design 10 years ago.

Overall, the park has undergone substantial redevelopment, with more recreational facilities added and existing ones expanded. Natural features such as the pond and woods have been removed, while new amenities including a café, toilets, and a picnic area have been introduced.

Ten years ago, the park contained relatively basic facilities. In the centre was a football pitch, with two tennis courts located to the north. To the east, there was a children’s play area, while a small car park for 20 vehicles lay to the west. In the southern part, a pond was surrounded by a wooded area.

At present, the park is more developed and better equipped. The number of tennis courts has doubled to four and remains in the northern section. The car park has been expanded significantly to accommodate 50 cars near the entrance. The children’s play area has been upgraded and divided into two zones: a standard play area and a soft play area, with toilets added nearby.

In the southern half, the pond and woods have been replaced by a large picnic area, and a café has been built in the southeast corner. A cycle path now runs along the bottom of the park, further improving accessibility.
